item.title: Radiant Capital - Rekt II
item.description: Radiant Capital gets a $53M haircut. Thought multi-sigs were safe? Think again. Radiant's "robust" 3/11 setup crumbled like a house of cards. Exploited twice in a year on Arbitrum, this multi-chain lender shows once more that opsec, not code, is often the weakest link.

item.title: Polter Finance
item.description: After losing roughly $8.7 million to a textbook case of oracle manipulation, Polter Finance is scrambling to clean up the mess. Their unaudited protocol left key contracts wide open on Fantom, and an attacker walked away rich.

item.title: DeltaPrime - Rekt II
item.description: Audited multiple times, hacked twice in two months. DeltaPrime loses another $4.85M after ignoring explicit warnings about admin key security. Like leaving your front door unlocked - on Arbitrum and Avalanche - in a bad neighborhood.

item.title: Tapioca DAO - Rekt
item.description: Another day, another private key theft, another protocol rekt. Tapioca DAO on Arbitrum suffers a roughly $4.4 million loss in a private key compromise. Some funds were partially recovered after negotiation with the attacker.

item.title: Penpie - Rekt
item.description: The crypto world never sleeps and neither do its hackers. In the latest episode of Who Wants to Be a Millionaire - DeFi Edition, Penpie has found itself on the wrong end of a $27 million reentrancy attack on Pendle markets, leaving Ethereum and Arbitrum users counting their losses.

item.title: LiFi/Jumper - Rekt
item.description: Infinite approvals... the ultimate leap of faith strikes again. LiFi protocol lost $9.73M to an attack draining addresses that had previously approved infinite spending on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon and Base.

item.title: Rho Market - Rekt
item.description: An oracle's misconfiguration turns into a $7.5 million windfall for an alert MEV bot. What began as a simple misstep in Rho Market's oracle configuration on Scroll turned into a six-figure five-figure-fee MEV bonanza.
